regions.text: In English we do not always say what we mean. 'How do you do?' and 'How are you?' are said on meeting. Sometimes people shake hands.
'How are you?' looks like a question, and sometimes it is a question about someone’s health.
meeting: coming together. shake hands: taking each other’s right hand. health: being well or ill. WHO was started in 1947.
FUTURE: will shake. PRESENT: shake(s). PAST: shook, shaken.
